sessions.description: Pharos bridging TradFi and decentralized systems enabling efficient asset tokenization and seamless onchain settlements.

sessions.title: The Future of Real Estate: Stablecoins, Onchain Settlement and RWAs
sessions.description: Real estate is moving onchain. This talk explores how stablecoins, RWAs, and DeFi are transforming property settlement, ownership, and access.\n

sessions.title: From Scarcity to Abundance: Financing the Multi-Trillion Dollar Infrastructure of the Future
sessions.description: New technologies are driving production costs toward zero, but scaling these industries requires enormous capital and new financial architecture -- something traditional finance struggles to provide.\nOnchain finance offers a different model: global liquidity, programmable lending markets, transparent risk, and collateral backed by real-world production.\nIn this talk, Stani explores how the next financial system will not just trade assets; it will fund the infrastructure of the future.

sessions.title: Solving The RWA Trilemma
sessions.description: A session on a key security challenge in scaling RWAs: exposure from shared pools. It covers how Euler’s modular vault architecture and the Euler Vault Kit give institutions flexible, isolated control over RWA credit markets.

sessions.title: The On-Chain Euro: An Open Design Conversation Between Banks and the Builders Who'll Make It Real
sessions.description: DeFi runs on dollars. That's a risk, not a feature. A consortium of major European banks is launching a DeFi-native euro stablecoin on Ethereum: here's the architecture, the strategy, and where builders come in. Bring your toughest questions!

sessions.title: MiCA Lookback: Regulation, Adoption, and Europe’s Crypto Crossroads
sessions.description: A practical look back at MiCA’s impact on the European crypto market—what regulation enabled, what it constrained, and whether it helped Europe scale crypto adoption while staying competitive globally.

sessions.title: From 5 Stablecoins to 1,000 Deployments: The Liquidity Problem Hiding in Plain Sight
sessions.description: There are now almost 1000 stablecoin deployments across ~50 chains. Most have almost no liquidity. Using onchain data, I'll show why this fragmentation is accelerating and what new infrastructure is needed to unify it.

sessions.title: Building Markets Surrounding Tokenized Assets that Can Reach Enterprise Scale.
sessions.description: This talk breaks down what it takes to move tokenization from small pilots to real institutional scale, focusing on regulated stablecoins, interoperable settlement rails and the infrastructure needed for global, on-chain financial products.

sessions.title: ERC-3643 Regulation
sessions.description: Provide an overview of adoption of ERC-3643 by the SEC and 23 jurisdictions around the world

sessions.title: Stablecoins: Payments, Politics and Power
sessions.description: The session explains the state of the stablecoin technology, why and how it is being increasingly adopted by real world companies from banks, through corp treasuries to stock exchanges and the important political context that affects its deployment.

sessions.title: DeFi's hidden risk stack
sessions.description: Mapping real DeFi risk stack from validator operations across networks to exploit analysis and design patterns.

sessions.description: Relaxed breakfast networking for founders and investors. Organized by Masterkey VC.

sessions.title: Credit for the Machine Age: Why DeFi's Missing Primitive Isn't Intelligence
sessions.description: Programmable credit opening new design space beyond overcollateralized lending enabling neo finance and agentic commerce.

sessions.title: From Retail to Institutional: Layer 2 Solutions for RWA Settlement
sessions.description: As RWA markets scale, we’ll dig into how Layer 2 setups (rollups and validiums) knit together into one seamless settlement backbone. Explore how non-custodial exchange layers enable atomic, compliant settlement for retail and institutional users.

sessions.title: Beyond Rewards: Incentives as Core Infrastructure in Modern DeFi
sessions.description: Incentives evolution from short-term rewards to core DeFi components driving liquidity, yield, and engagement.

sessions.title: Rate Discovery: The Key to Fixed-Rate Onchain Lending
sessions.description: DeFi lending has solved liquidity, risk isolation, and capital efficiency. The last missing piece is pricing. This talk explores why formula-driven rates hit a ceiling and how market-driven rate discovery unlocks the next era of onchain credit.

sessions.title: Lido stVaults and Earn: Staking Infra That Powers DeFi Yields
sessions.description: A walkthrough of how Lido V3 and Lido Earn unlock potential for institutional adoption of staking and DeFi on Ethereum.

sessions.title: Tokenized Funds in Europe: The 3 Decisions That Separate Pilots from Production
sessions.description: Why tokenized fund pilots stall in Europe and the 3 decisions that unlock production: governance model, custody architecture, and value chain integration.

sessions.title: From Idleness to Productivity: Building Institutional-Grade Yield System for DeFi at Scale
sessions.description: DeFi’s next growth wave needs better “yield rails” not more protocols. This talk explores how infrastructure turns DeFi yield into programmable, scalable strategies for wallets, custodians, fintechs, and institutions.

sessions.title: The Metamorphosis of DeFi: The Herd Is Finally Here
sessions.description: BitGo is spreading its wings and ready to take flight: \n\nThe time is now. DeFi must evolve to match the unprecedented institutional growth over the past 18 months.

sessions.title: The MetaDEX Thesis: Rewriting the Rules of DeFi
sessions.description: MetaDEXes redefine DeFi with incentive systems that align traders, builders, and liquidity providers. This talk explores how new market architectures can make DeFi more generative, efficient, and fair.

sessions.title: The New Frontier of Finance: Institutional Adoption of On-Chain Opportunities
sessions.description: As the lines between TradFi and Web3 blur, institutions are moving on-chain. This session explores the motivations, challenges, and frameworks enabling their responsible blockchain adoption.

sessions.title: Stop Using Ranges to Concentrate Liquidity in a Pool
sessions.description: Since Uniswap v3, you can concentrate liquidity in a pool over token price at a granular level called ticks. As an LP, we solve for liquidity across ticks using vectors, not ranges. Our results resemble bimodal bid-ask spreads from order books.

sessions.title: The Real AI Gold Rush: Tokenizing the Shovels, Not the Hype
sessions.description: Everyone is chasing the AI boom. But the real opportunity isn’t in the models, it’s in the compute that powers them. Tokenizing GPU infrastructure is creating the next wave of real-world yield in DeFi.

sessions.title: Building Corridors to Power Stablecoin Flows
sessions.description: Fast, functional corridors are a requirement to capture the utility that stablecoins promise. We share our experiences and best practices in building corridors globally, including LatAm, Asia, and the Middle East.

sessions.title: October 10th: The Day ADL Broke — What the Crash Revealed About Perp DEX Risk Models
sessions.description: The 10/10 crypto crash exposed the structural flaw hidden in risk engines across major perp DEXs: cross-margin PnL. I’ll present a more robust risk mechanism that makes ADL more predictable for traders in a new design paradigm called Hybrid Margin.

sessions.title: The Rise of Composable RWAs
sessions.description: RWAs are shifting from static tokenized assets to primitives of DeFi. Using onchain data from Dune, we explore how RWA protocols are integrating with lending, yield, and liquidity layers across chains.

sessions.title: Liquid Apps for a Connected Onchain Economy
sessions.description: Liquid Apps are a new class of Web3 applications that eliminate all onchain friction, enabling users to interact with any app across the EVM ecosystem, with a single wallet and seamless liquidity. It enables a truly connected onchain economy.

sessions.title: When regulation meets vision: what the EU should learn from the US
sessions.description: Europe regulated crypto first, but the US is now embracing it strategically. This talk compares both approaches and explores how Europe can move from defensive regulation to offensive policymaking to empower the next wave of financial innovation.

sessions.title: From Protocols to Products: Turning Digital Asset Innovation Into Institutional Reality
sessions.description: Evolution of financial protocols into institution-ready products through trusted controls, secure custody, and compliant frameworks.

sessions.title: DeFi From the Inside: What Power Users Reveal About the Next Wave of Adoption
sessions.description: Power user perspective on DeFi revealing what works, breaks consistently, and points to real adoption stages.

sessions.description: Treating regulation as a foundational layer enables compliant institutional adoption, investor protection, and sustainable growth across global digital asset markets.

sessions.description: RWAs are redefining Web3, but static user personas can’t capture human complexity. This talk introduces the Human Orbit Model: A behavioral framework for designing inclusive, adaptive, and systematic UX that evolve with users.

sessions.title: Liquidated by My Own Code
sessions.description: What using a CDP stablecoin for two years actually looks like, including getting liquidated by your own code. Lessons from free floating pegs, why decentralized stablecoins matter at any scale, and why 30 people agreeing on money is still money.

sessions.title: What's Next for DeFi UX: Behavior-based Systems for the RWA Era
sessions.description: Human Orbit Model for designing inclusive, adaptive, and systematic UX evolving with users in the RWA environment.

sessions.title: From Regulated Stablecoins to DeFi : Bridging Institutional Finance and Ethereum

sessions.title: Designing Vault Stacks: The Stream Finance Wake-Up Call
sessions.description: Stream Finance showed how managed vaults can fail. This talk distills the lessons into a practical “vault stack” blueprint: onchain mandates, aligned incentives, hard risk limits, live NAV/exposure reporting, audits, and institutional-grade ops

sessions.description: DeFi’s reliance on USD poses systemic risk as global politics shift. Gísli shares lessons from building a euro stablecoin, focusing on distribution, liquidity, yield, and how wallets, solvers, neobanks, and issuers make the euro viable in DeFi.

sessions.description: Ethereum is emerging as a global settlement layer for onchain finance. This talk explores how Ethereum leverages the Chainlink standard to power DeFi, interoperability, and tokenized assets—connecting real-world markets to secure onchain settlement.

sessions.description: Fully onchain Parallel AMM delivering near-zero slippage, reduced impermanent loss, and linear scalability with 7× throughput.

sessions.description: UX and design principles making privacy tools accessible and effortless for everyday users, not just tech-savvy ones.

sessions.title: Keeping your users' private parts private
sessions.description: Patterns for product thinking and engineering preventing user doxxing while building with privacy-first approaches.

sessions.description: Contract-defined sequencing rules controlling transaction ordering and execution essential for customizable L2 systems.

sessions.description: This talk presents a fast algorithm for finding optimal arbitrage across many AMMs. By reducing convex optimization to marginal-price root finding, it achieves up to 200× speedups and works reliably with concentrated and leveraged liquidity.

sessions.title: Why marketing isn't enough anymore
sessions.description: We've seen multiple ways for chains and protocols to market to their audiences: KOLs, InfoFi, Research. Today it's not enough anymore. This talk will focus on how protocols and chains can maintain their user base even after a marketing campaign.

sessions.title: Blockchain for good
sessions.description: How are blockchain, tokens, and decentralized tools actually being used today to reshape humanitarian crisis response and development aid across the globe? ​Meet a few NGOs and take up their crypto challenge!

After working with 200+ Web3 brands, Flight3 CEO Harry Horsfall breaks down why most crypto marketing fails and what projects must do differently. In a market flooded with tokens, jargon and infinite AI slop, the projects that win won’t be the most technical, they’ll be the best and most creative storytellers. This keynote explores how the best teams turn complex infrastructure into simple, human stories that cut through and reach beyond the crypto bubble.

sessions.title: Sedna: How users can enforce affordable privacy censorship-resistance and privacy in MCP chains.
sessions.description: Sedna replaces naive transaction replication in multi-proposer blockchains with rateless erasure coding, achieving 2-3× bandwidth efficiency while preserving censorship resistance and reducing MEV exposure through until-decode privacy.

sessions.title: How Ethereum Can Scale to Billions of Users
sessions.description: Layer 2 advancements, security-preserving protocols, developer-friendly designs enabling large-scale adoption.

sessions.title: The Hardware Barrier: How to Onboard Next 100M to Self Custody
sessions.description: Security is no longer a flex—it’s the baseline. To onboard the next 100M, hardware must adopt the versatility of neobanks. This session explores the UX shift from cold storage to active utility.

sessions.title: Keccacheck: towards a SNARK friendly Keccak
sessions.description: Demonstration of a new GKR-based protocol combined with groth16 to bring down the average cost of a Keccak instance to <4k R1CS constraints.

sessions.title: Turning an EIP Into a Rallying Point for the Ecosystem
sessions.description: This talk shares what it’s like from a marketer’s POV to pull people together around an EIP to encrypt Ethereum's mempool while it's still taking shape. Ideally told after inclusion, but hey! It's a live case study on de-jargoning ideas, balancing education/memes in real-time.

sessions.title: Primary AMMs: Designing Financially Composable Tokens at Issuance
sessions.description: This talk examines how primary AMMs, where the protocol operates as a continuous counterparty, transform tokens from passive units of account into self-contained financial systems with native market structure, backing and capital utility.

sessions.title: You are not alone
sessions.description: Ethereum teams keep reinventing indexing, analytics & monitoring. Cambrian turns on-chain events + standards (ERC-20, etc.) into ready-to-query datasets—plus a chatbot that converts a Solidity repo into a DeFi analytics page.

sessions.title: Aave V4 and Beyond: Bringing Capital Markets Onchain
sessions.description: Deep dive into Aave V4 architecture enabling new borrowing strategies bridging offchain and onchain financial systems.

sessions.title: End of Beginning of DAO
sessions.description: Minimized DAOs unable to defend token holders, exploring power balance for protocol governance.

sessions.title: Deep dive into Native Rollups - Spec & Implementation
sessions.description: Native rollups have been proposed as the eventual destination stage for L2s. In this talk, we will deep dive into its spec and development with insights from our (ZEAM) work on prototyping it.

sessions.title: Designing Coherence: Aligning Ethereum Stakeholders Through Physical Environments
sessions.description: Ethereum products often struggle not because of tech, but misalignment between builders, users, and institutions. This talk shows how physical environments and narrative framing help stakeholders meet at coherence rather than compromise.

sessions.title: Sovereign Appchains: The Next Evolution of Scalable and Interoperable Web3 Infrastructure
sessions.description: Sovereign appchains offer a new path for scalable and reliable Web3 infrastructure. This session explains how app-specific chains reduce fragmentation, remove dependency on L2 ecosystems, and enable safe, native interoperability across major networks

sessions.title: CMTAT: Engineering a Security Token on EVM, with a Path to Privacy
sessions.description: How can real-world assets be represented on-chain? This talk reviews CMTAT, a security token framework for regulated instruments, covering design choices, standards, and technical decisions for Ethereum and EVM-compatible blockchains.

sessions.title: Matcha Meta: Building the Next Generation of Cross-Chain Trading
sessions.description: A live demo of Matcha Meta, a next-gen meta-aggregator for same- and cross-chain trading. We’ll show how multi-chain routing, quote simulation, and execution controls unlock better UX, security, and performance for DeFi users.

sessions.title: AI as a Curator Layer for DeFi Lending: From Static Rules to Adaptive Risk Management
sessions.description: Decentralized lending still relies on static interest-rate rules. This talk shows how AI and reinforcement learning can act as a control layer for DeFi lending, dynamically adjusting rates and risk parameters using on-chain data and stress scenarios

sessions.title: Owning the Past: Building Permanent Memory
sessions.description: Memory Protocol is building the foundation for portable, user-owned data. This talk explores how it works, what’s being built on it today, and how personal memory could reshape the internet in the coming years and decades.

sessions.title: Economic Security in Optimistic-Style Challenges
sessions.description: Are challenge-based optimistic protocols truly incentive safe? I highlight failure of single-winner designs, and examine scenarios where partial burn and multi-winner rewards best align honest participation without overreliance on assumptions.

sessions.title: Consumer RWA: Tokenizing Everyday Items for Mass Adoption
sessions.description: ivault turns household goods into yield-bearing assets. Discover the P2P marketplace bringing mass adoption to RWA, already used by 155k+ people worldwide.

sessions.title: Rollups Broke Ethereum. Synchronous Composability Can Fix It.
sessions.description: Why asynchronous interoperability cannot replace synchronous composability with applications breaking without it, restoration paths.

sessions.title: Increasing Global Access to Credit Using Zero Knowledge Proofs
sessions.description: Millions of people earn online but cannot access credit due to lack of verifiable financial data. This talk explores how zero knowledge proofs and real time platform data can unlock credit access and enable uncollateralized crypto lending.

sessions.title: Stop Selling Blockchain.
sessions.description: How to position a crypto company in 2026 without sounding like crypto.

sessions.title: Fast finality is essential for mass adoption
sessions.description: Fast finality of the L1 is essential and EIL is insufficient for L2 interoperability. Moreover, L2 interoperability is essential for mass adoption.

sessions.title: Encryption Day
sessions.description: Full day on fully homomorphic encryption (FHE) and its applications. Organized by Fhenix.

sessions.title: The Secret Garden
sessions.description: Intimate networking event by Quantstamp and Zircuit. Organized by Quantstamp, Zircuit.

sessions.title: Introducing web3:// – Transforming the EVM into an Unstoppable Decentralized Web Server
sessions.description: This talk introduces web3:// (ERC-4804/6860), a decentralized access protocol that enables HTTP-style links to on-chain resources, transforming the EVM into an unstoppable web server and eliminating reliance on DNS and centralized hosting.

sessions.title: Proofs as a smart contract primitives
sessions.description: As protocol-level proof verification becomes cheap and easy to use, proofs move from being a scaling tool to an application primitive. This talk explores what new design spaces open up for smart contract developers in that paradigm

sessions.title: Privacy as Infrastructure: What Institutions Actually Need on Ethereum
sessions.description: Why do institutions need privacy on public chains? The Institutional Privacy Task Force (Ethereum Foundation) presents our open market map: validated use cases, privacy patterns, and vendor solutions for building on Ethereum.

sessions.title: Scaling Ethereum with zkVM
sessions.description: I'll explain how real-time proving can boost Ethereum throughput by linking native rollups with synchronous composability and enabling asynchronous messaging between non-native rollups. Plus, an update on the ZisK project.

sessions.title: From Builder to Leader Identity: The Metamorphosis No One Talks About
sessions.description: Developers thrive on fast dopamine loops: shipping, fixing, shipping again. Founders get ambiguity and no feedback. Burnout isn’t about grit; it’s neurobiology. I’ll show how “builder” and “leader” modes can coexist so founders don’t lose themselves

sessions.title: Futarchy on Ethereum: Practical Learnings from the Kleros DAO
sessions.description: Kleros DAO experiment with Futarchy.fi: real proposals and real trades on $PNK. Markets gave governance signals, blocked bad ideas, and let holders hedge their positions. Come learn the practical benefits of Futarchy made in Ethereum!

sessions.title: How to do Web3 marketing without actually doing any marketing
sessions.description: Do marketings stunts really work and do they achieve marketing goals without necessarily being direct marketing? Let's look at the most prominent examples from the Web3 industry.

sessions.title: Who Controls Economic Truth on Ethereum?
sessions.description: As DeFi begins to rely on macro and real world data, control over data becomes control over outcomes. This talk examines oracle design, censorship resistance and who ultimately defines economic truth on Ethereum.

sessions.title: On Chain Privacy: What’s Different For Enterprises
sessions.description: How to pick between all the different privacy solutions out there today - especially given the unique needs of business users.

sessions.title: ZK Is Not Enough: Privacy Requires System (Re)Design
sessions.description: Zero-knowledge proofs alone don’t guarantee privacy. We'll explore in this talk why real privacy requires system-level redesign: seamless UX, automated & time-decoupled transactions, and compliance-aware architectures that work together in practice.

sessions.title: Programmable Native Bitcoin for Everyday Finance
sessions.description: This talk shows how native Bitcoin can be programmed for use across DeFi and traditional financial products, enabling everyday utility without wrapping or compromising self custody, and shifting Bitcoin from something you hold to something you use.

sessions.title: Legal & Compliance
sessions.description: DeFi meets TradFi exploration of governance, compliance, privacy in converging markets with regulatory clarity focus.

sessions.title: Regenerative Finance
sessions.description: ReFi leverages Web3 to drive positive environmental and social change. Join Giveth, Celo, and other leaders to discuss real-world impact, explore emerging solutions, and collaborate on meaningful progress.

sessions.title: Voting and Vote Splitting in Crypto-economic Systems
sessions.description: We consider how vote splitting between similar options manifests itself with subtle differences in settings such as governance voting in DAOs, voting in Schelling-point oracles such as Kleros and UMA, and choosing a fork in a consensus algorithm.

sessions.title: W3ST - Web3 Security Tools Seminar
sessions.description: Web3 Security Tools seminar — hands-on security tooling workshops. Organized by W3ST.

sessions.title: Hardware wallet integration of Privacy preserving protocols for the Kohaku wallet
sessions.description: Optimized and clear signed implementation of privacy protocols in hardware wallets expanding adoption.

sessions.title: Blockchain trilemma cracked: what about the stablecoin's?
sessions.description: Vitalik Buterin announced that Ethereum finally cracked the blockchain trilemma. At the same time, Stablecoins, one of the main uses of Ethereum, are 99.5% made of trusted and centralized iterations.\nCould the stablecoin trilemma also be solved?

sessions.title: Building the Verifiable Attention Economy
sessions.description: UGC campaigns often rely on surface metrics that fail to show who actually drives value. \nThis talk explains how verifiable attention uses AI and crypto to measure real contribution and give brands a more reliable way to run community-led growth.

sessions.title: New Media Arc: Storytelling to drive Ethereum Adoption
sessions.description: This fireside chat with Dayana, Louie (EF), and Binji (EF) focuses on how to tell the human stories behind projects to increase use cases, build a brand, get funded, and expand product reach. Ideal for tech and non-tech audiences.

sessions.title: Cooking With GASS: A Developer-Friendly Airdrop Mechanism
sessions.description: Github Activity Scoring System is an open source PoC that uses Forte’s rule engine and onchain oracle to bring Web2 dev data onchain, scoring real contributions and unlocking fair, transparent airdrops that feel ETH-native.

sessions.title: Smart contract identity for orgs and agents
sessions.description: What if every smart contract and wallet in your organisation had a clear identity that could be managed by both humans and AI agents? Enscribe brings identity to onchain systems using ENS. We’ll demo how this enables teams and agents to easily manage contracts, wallets, and organisational structure onchain.

sessions.title: Multisig Confidential: A Multisig Smart Account Powered by Fully Homomorphic Encryption
sessions.description: Smart accounts (ERC-4337) enable programmable wallets, and Fully Homomorphic Encryption (FHE) enables private computation on encrypted data. This talk presents the design and implementation of confidential multisig modules for ERC-7579 smart accounts

sessions.title: Profiting from Privacy
sessions.description: Privacy is not a trade-off, but an opportunity. This talk explores how privacy can create real economic value, reshape incentives, and open new paths for sustainable Web3 businesses.

sessions.title: Confidential Tokens: The Missing Primitive for the Next Generation of DeFi
sessions.description: DeFi tokens are transparent by default, limiting real-world and institutional adoption. This talk introduces Confidential Tokens, a new asset primitive combining TEE, MPC, and Zero-Knowledge to bring privacy to DeFi.

sessions.title: How ZK Identity and Account Abstraction Are the Only Firewall Against Government Surveillance of AI
sessions.description: AI prompts are seizable by governments. ZK Identity and Account Abstraction are the only defense. We detail a cryptographically-enforced firewall that decouples your self-sovereign identity from your AI data, making seizure technically infeasible.

sessions.title: Launching a rollup is the easy part
sessions.description: Infrastructure, integrations, protocols, partnerships, wallets, liquidity... deploying the contracts is the tip of the iceberg. Running a rollup is a coordination game all on its own. Making this as simple as possible is key to global adoption.

sessions.title: Aktionariat: Secondary Markets on Ethereum without Intermediaries
sessions.description: Using Aktionariat to issue tokenized equity on Ethereum, receive direct investment and enabling secondary trading.

sessions.title: Build micropayment x402 solutions for communities
sessions.description: From developer for developers, let do a workshop to create or adapt existing services into x402 payments for community services

sessions.title: Economic Censorship Games in Fraud Proofs
sessions.description: Economic censorship in scenarios requiring sequential transactions by honest parties in fraud proof systems.

sessions.title: Cypherpunk and Decentralized Social
sessions.description: This presentation shows how Lens and Mask turn cypherpunk ideals into reality, proving decentralized social can scale, empower creators, and give users ownership, transforming the internet from platform control to user-built culture.

sessions.title: We are playing a game bigger than us
sessions.description: We often talk about crypto as if it were just technology: better UX, better wallets, better governance. But the environment around us has changed, we are not the little league anymore. How shall we adapt?

sessions.title: Gm gm is Not Internal Comms, Peeps. BUIDLing Web3 Culture
sessions.description: In Web3 communicating with your team members is critical. A battle tested comms badass will share internal comms strategies for decentralized teams to survive & thrive. From WTF (What The Feedback) to WAGMI, learn how to fix internal comms chaos.

sessions.title: To Incentivize or Not: Impact of Blockchain-Based Cryptoeconomic Tokens on Human Information Sharing
sessions.description: Do tokens really boost data sharing? We ran a 132-person experiment on payment + reputation tokens. See what increases useful, accurate, contextual info (and what kills motivation), plus a surprising two-token interaction.

sessions.title: How Creators Finally Get Paid Instantly Onchain
sessions.description: Creators still deal with slow payouts, high fees, and fragmented tools. In this talk, I share what I learned building LID and working with early creators and how onchain rails like smart wallets and instant USDC payouts unlock a better income model.

sessions.title: OpenSigner: Open-source non-custodial key management
sessions.description: Secret sharing approach splitting keys into three shares enabling secure, self-hostable key management without complexity.

sessions.title: Web3 Is Alive: Onboarding the 99.1% with the Human Layer
sessions.description: Last year I argued Web3 is alive. This year I’ll show how it scales to the 99.1%: passkeys + smart accounts (account abstraction), gasless interactions, onchain identity/permissions, and dynamic passes/quests that connect real actions to verifiable state. The key upgrade is packaging these flows as a protocol + SDK so partners (apps, events, brands) can integrate a repeatable “mainstream UX” stack. I’ll include a short live walkthrough to make it tangible and highlight the real trade-offs.

sessions.title: Public Chains, Private Guarantees: Designing Layer 2s for Institutional Settlement
sessions.description: Layer 2s are evolving from scaling tools into settlement infrastructure. This talk explores how rollups can offer institutional-grade guarantees on public Ethereum without reverting to permissioned chains or fragmented architectures.

sessions.title: Europe’s Next Digital Needs and How Ethereum Can Meet Them
sessions.description: Europe is rolling out huge digital reforms that create entirely new product needs. This talk shows builders which real-world problems EU policies are creating and how Ethereum projects can design the tools, infrastructure and experiences institutions need.

sessions.title: Freedom: A Browser for the Decentralized Web
sessions.description: Freedom is a desktop browser for the decentralized web that treats Swarm, IPFS, and ENS as first-class protocols. It ships with integrated peer-to-peer nodes, enabling direct network access without relying on centralized gateways.

sessions.title: Web3 Is Alive: The Human Layer in Action (Live Demo)
sessions.description: Screen-mirrored demo of Human Layer with account abstraction, passkeys, gasless actions, onchain identity, and dynamic passes.

sessions.title: The Cost of Entry
sessions.description: What happens when research gets crowded out by hype? This panel explores why blockchain still needs spaces built for rigorous ideas, sharp critique, and real debate, and how TERSE brings a research-first forum into one of the largest crypto events.

sessions.title: Spritz — Building a Truly Decentralized, Censorship-Resistant and Permissionless Communication App
sessions.description: Open-source communication platform using ENS, Logos, and E2E encryption delivering censorship-resistant chat and streaming.

sessions.title: Updates on Ethereum Interoperability
sessions.description: Recent and future launches of interoperability efforts making Ethereum feel like a single network.

sessions.title: Privacy and self-sovereign infrastructure!
sessions.description: Self-sovereign infrastructure as answer to heterogeneous bifurcated AI landscape protecting against censorship.

sessions.title: Synchronously composable L2s today, not tomorrow
sessions.description: Stage 2 trustless synchronous composability between L2s and L1 is achievable today using ZK real-time proving without compromising on trustlessness or user fund security.

sessions.title: Decentralized Social Update: Embracing Too Much, Holding Too Little
sessions.description: Decentralized social is back — but not in the way we expected. \nWallets are embracing social features, while social platforms are moving closer to becoming wallets. But does this bring us closer to better communication, or just reshape old problems?

sessions.title: From product to adoption: how privacy becomes the default
sessions.description: A product-focused walkthrough of adoption & integration pathways (user acquisition, SDK and deployments), a look at the TAM for privacy vs. what's been achieved. How to find PMF for early stage products, and a look at the north star for privacy infra

sessions.title: Scaling DeFi: Cannes Edition
sessions.description: Panel and discussion on scaling DeFi infrastructure. Organized by Kpk.

sessions.title: La Lumiere w/ Wirex, Privy & Bridge
sessions.description: Multi-partner social event by Wirex, Privy, and Bridge. Organized by Wirex, Privy, Bridge.

sessions.title: Mezo Devs Rooftop Happy Hour
sessions.description: Rooftop happy hour for developers by Mezo. Organized by Mezo.

sessions.title: Blockchain Geopolitics: Decolonizing Cyberspace in the Age of Surveillance
sessions.description: With the rise of network states, blockchains have become geopolitical territory. They can enable democratic coordination as well as authoritarian control. This talk explores blockchain as a counter-infrastructure for individual sovereignty.

sessions.title: Arc: Bringing the offchain onchain
sessions.description: How Arc is designing blockspace for real financial activity: USDC as gas, sub-second finality, and compliant privacy. A practical look at bringing payments, trading, and RWAs onchain without fragmenting liquidity.

sessions.title: How We Solved Anonymous Credibility for Whistleblowers
sessions.description: Whistleblowers face an impossible choice: stay anonymous and get ignored, or verify themselves and face retaliation. zkWhistleblower uses zero-knowledge proofs to enable cryptographic verification of credibility without identity disclosure.

sessions.title: From Experiments to Adoption: Why AI Agent Products Struggle With Monetization and GTM
sessions.description: Many AI agent products show strong technical progress but fail to reach product-market fit. Gartner estimates 40% of AI projects will be canceled by 2027 due to unclear business value. This talk explores why positioning, pricing, and GTM break down.

sessions.title: building a d/combinator
sessions.description: Ethereum has capital, but lacks a repeatable engine for durable teams. I’ll present a Swiss-built d/combinator for d/acc: privacy-first cohorts + pop-up villages feeding a permanent neutral hub that compounds alumni, trust, and throughput.

sessions.title: What is the Next Evolution for Layer 2s?
sessions.description: The talk will explore the evolution of L2 networks on ETH, and how technologies are advancing to meet market needs and conditions. This session will discuss UX improvements, scalability and more, as L2s vie for market share

sessions.title: The AI Detection Illusion: Why “More Findings” Is a Terrible Go-To-Market Metric
sessions.description: AI security tools sell “more findings,” but volume misleads—creating noise and false confidence. Learn why this GTM narrative persists, how it warps evaluation, and which metrics actually predict real workflow impact.

sessions.title: SunDAO: Investors & Builders Mixer
sessions.description: Investors and builders mixer by SunDAO Ventures. Organized by SunDAO Ventures.

sessions.title: After Hours with Rain & Turnkey
sessions.description: Evening networking by Rain and Turnkey. Organized by Rain, Turnkey.

sessions.title: Starknet Party
sessions.description: Beach party at La Plage Du Festival for the Starknet community. Organized by StarkWare, Starknet Foundation.

sessions.title: RedStone Bolt Run
sessions.description: Morning running event by RedStone. Organized by RedStone.

sessions.title: Physical & Operational Security Breakfast
sessions.description: Breakfast seminar on physical and operational security for crypto founders. Organized by Opsek.

sessions.title: Hack Seasons Conference
sessions.description: Full-day conference covering DeFi, infrastructure, and emerging narratives. Organized by Hack Seasons.

sessions.title: The Early Days of Ethereum
sessions.description: Social history project preserving stories of hundreds involved with Ethereum from earliest days with videos and anecdotes.

sessions.title: Sovereign Agents are here: Local-First AI with Trustless Identity, Micropayments, and Private Data
sessions.description: Run Local-first AI agents in your hardware. The stack is here: keep data private locally, discover each other via ERC-8004, and transact through micropayments. Not your weights, not your brain; not your data, not your agent.

sessions.title: Designing City Pilots
sessions.description: Historically, new cities took 50 years or more to emerge. With lessons from botanics, cybernetics, and human-centered design, we will explore how to create web3-native cities in the next 3-5 years.

sessions.title: From Stars to Bits : Space Infrastructure for Safe Computation
sessions.description: This talk will cover how our space infrastructure (orbitport) can enable trustworthy applications, such as KMS and true random number generation and verification, including live demos of our sdk and threshold ecdsa integration

sessions.title: Proving Ethereum in Real-Time With OpenVM 2.0
sessions.description: This talk explains how OpenVM 2.0 enables real time proving for Ethereum using the new SWIRL ZK proof system. We discuss performance benchmarks, the core OpenVM architecture, and implications for developers and Ethereum's ZK-based scaling roadmap.

sessions.title: Vault Summit
sessions.description: Summit on vault strategies and yield optimization by Morpho. Organized by Morpho.

sessions.title: Cannes Walking Tour (Apr 1)
sessions.description: Guided walking tour of Cannes for the Ethereum community. Organized by Ambire.

sessions.title: The first 100 days of Fusaka and the future of PeerDAS
sessions.description: This talk presents an analysis of the first 100 days of the Ethereum Fusaka hard fork, based on real-world PeerDAS mainnet data. We examine PeerDAS performance and data availability, discuss partial messages, EIP-8077, and the sharded blob mempool, and explain the future of PeerDAS and why 2D encoding is needed.

sessions.title: Money: Free speech?
sessions.description: Block Fighters comes back on La Croisette this year again, a fun, satirical debate format where two speakers with opposing views face off on a boxing-ring style stage, moderated by a judge. It's designed to spark lively discussions on key industry issues.

sessions.title: Educating Developers, What can we Learn from Experimental Schools?
sessions.description: Exploring new US education models—spaced repetition, tutoring, neuroscience methods—reshaping developer learning in AI-driven world.

sessions.title: From MEV-Boost to ePBS: The Protocolization of Proposer-Builder Separation
sessions.description: Ethereum's journey toward proposer builder separation enhancing scalability, streamlining MEV markets, unlocking execution scaling.

sessions.title: The Token Trap: Decentralization’s Kryptonite
sessions.description: Is decentralization in crypto broken? After 14 years here I've grown deeply skeptical of tokens as the primary coordination mechanism. Come hear why they’ve exhausted their utility and what I think comes next for decentralization, especially for AI.

sessions.title: Quantum dilema
sessions.description: Satirical debate on quantum computing dilemma with opposing views sparking lively crypto industry discussions.

sessions.title: VOPS / Partial Stateless nodes
sessions.description: Exploring Verkle-Optimized Partial Sync (VOPS) and partial stateless node designs to reduce Ethereum validator hardware requirements while maintaining full verification capabilities. Learn how these approaches can lower the barrier to solo staking and improve network decentralization.

sessions.title: ZK Attesting
sessions.description: zkAttester clients pave the way towards significantly reducing hardware requirements & sync times. Attesters verify ZK proofs, eliminating re-execution. This unlocks massive L1-scaling while fostering a leaner, decentralized network.

sessions.title: Post Quantum Transaction Signatures for Ethereum One Year Later
sessions.description: A year ago post quantum transaction signatures for Ethereum were largely a theoretical exercise. Since then the conversation has shifted toward concrete engineering clearer requirements and realistic deployment paths.\n\nThis talk presents a one year check in on what changed what assumptions broke and what now looks shippable. A central outcome is the role of Account Abstraction AA as the enabling layer for post quantum signing both today via non native AA and as the long term destination via native AA. This dual path allows real world experimentation now while converging toward protocol level support.

sessions.title: There is such a thing as a free lunch - the deep fake refund economy
sessions.description: Fraud rise in food delivery refunds with generative AI using media provenance and onchain risk transfer to combat.

sessions.title: Proving Less, Verifying More: 3.5× Faster L2 Validity Proofs
sessions.description: Sparse techniques cutting L2 state derivation costs by 6.5× (3.5× overall) using Optimism as example.

sessions.title: From 30M to 300M: The Engineering Behind Ethereum’s Gas Limit Leap
sessions.description: Ethereum’s gas limit has barely moved in years, not for lack of demand, but because execution, networking, and validation were bottlenecks. This talk walks through the engineering work that’s changing that.

sessions.title: Ethereum Treasury Company VS ETF
sessions.description: Block Fighters comes back on La Croisette this year again, a fun, satirical debate format where two speakers with opposing views face off on a boxing-ring style stage, moderated by a judge. It's designed to spark lively discussions on key industry issues.

sessions.title: Deep dive into Devp2p and how you can implement it
sessions.description: This workshop is about understanding the most important protocol of Ethereum : Devp2p. Devp2p is the p2p network protocol used by Ethereum nodes to communicate between them. We will learn about it and also how to implement it.

sessions.title: Investor x Founder Beachclub
sessions.description: Beach networking for investors and founders. Organized by Arrakis, Bitpanda, Uniswap.

sessions.title: THE LOCAL: Rooftop Brunch
sessions.description: Rooftop brunch for founders, funders, and friends. Organized by LO:Tech.

sessions.title: Token Engineering Breakout Session
sessions.description: Hands-on token engineering workshop. Organized by Token Engineering Academy, Bancor, UZH.

sessions.title: AI Agents Will Replace Dapps
sessions.description: Self-sovereign AI agents as superior smart contract interfaces offering safer, more censorship-resistant user experiences.

sessions.title: ETH issuance clash
sessions.description: Satirical debate on ETH issuance with opposing speakers on boxing-ring stage sparking lively industry discussions.

sessions.title: The Future of the Agent Economy: How Protocols Like x402 and ERC-8004 Will Build an Autonomous World
sessions.description: Join a dynamic panel to explore the agent economy's explosive growth, where AI agents transact autonomously via x402 payments and ERC-8004 trust registries. We'll debate how these protocols enable a $30T+ market by 2030, outscaling human economies.

sessions.title: EIP-7702 meets hardware wallets: stories from the browser extension wallet frontlines
sessions.description: Candid discussion about wrestling with hardware wallet friction, whitelist politics, ecosystem misalignment in EIP-7702 shipping.

sessions.title: Reputation Based Lending using decentralized Ai risk assessors and Social Graphs
sessions.description: This presentation explores how decentralized AI agents and social graph analysis can unlock a new model for reputation based lending. Instead of relying on traditional credit scores, decentralized AI risk assessors evaluate a borrower’s trust network

sessions.title: ePBS: What EthStakers Need to Know
sessions.description: ePBS is set to reshape how block building and proposal work in Ethereum. This talk breaks down how ePBS functions in practice and what it will mean for stakers, including changes to validator responsibilities, interactions with builders and relays, and day-to-day operations. It focuses on giving home stakers a clear, practical understanding of how to navigate and operate within an ePBS-enabled network.

sessions.title: Privacy delusion
sessions.description: Block Fighters comes back on La Croisette this year again, a fun, satirical debate format where two speakers with opposing views face off on a boxing-ring style stage, moderated by a judge. It's designed to spark lively discussions on key industry issues.

sessions.title: Financial Services in the Agent Economy: From Platforms to Autonomous Operating Systems
sessions.description: Four-layer fintech model shifting from interface to workflow economy via agentic automation and digital asset intersection.

sessions.title: DeFi Agents You Can Actually Trust
sessions.description: Verifiable yield agents, guardrails, ERC-8004 rebuilding trust in opaque agents addressing TVL collapse.

sessions.title: FOCIL: Past, Present and Future
sessions.description: FOCIL is an upcoming protocol change expected in the Hegota network upgrade. This talk covers how FOCIL came about, how it’s designed to work, and what it will mean for validators in practice. It also looks ahead to future directions like blob transaction support and potential integration with encrypted mempools, giving stakers a clear view of what to expect.

sessions.title: Autonomous On-Chain Intelligence: How AI Agents Will Transform Web3 User Behavior & Payments
sessions.description: Autonomous intelligence analyzing user behavior, predicting churn, personalizing payments across Ethereum using NASA-inspired methods.

sessions.title: Raising the Bar: A Deep Dive into Institutional Ethereum Staking Infrastructure
sessions.description: Bitwise institutional staking blueprint with multi-client redundancy and threshold key management for security.

sessions.title: Canton Network: sleeping giant?
sessions.description: Fun satirical debate format with opposing views on boxing-ring style stage, designed to spark lively discussions on industry issues.

sessions.title: Home staking behind CGNAT, LATAM, Asia-Pacific, and everywhere
sessions.description: Running a home staking node behind CGNAT presents real challenges for peer-to-peer connectivity, limiting inbound connections and weakening network participation. This setup is increasingly common across Latin America, Asia-Pacific, and Africa, and is steadily expanding into Europe and North America. This talk explains why CGNAT creates peering issues at both the consensus and execution layers, and how dual-stack (IPv4 + IPv6) connectivity provides a practical and effective solution. It covers the current state of client support, highlighting implementations that work well today, and walks through how home stakers can set up dual-stack networking to improve peer connectivity and resilience.

sessions.title: $$1B across 7 blockchains: deep dive into the tech architecture of Europe’s largest RWA issuer
sessions.description: Spiko manages over $1B in RWA across 7 blockchains. In this talk, we share the smart contract architecture and multi-chain infrastructure that allow us to operate high-throughput, regulated financial products at scale.

sessions.title: Accelerating Ethereum

sessions.title: AI against AI - find the angle
sessions.description: Block Fighters comes back on La Croisette this year again, a fun, satirical debate format where two speakers with opposing views face off on a boxing-ring style stage, moderated by a judge. It's designed to spark lively discussions on key industry issues.

sessions.title: Validator Redirected Revenue
sessions.description:
This talk opens a conversation on 2 execution client level changes:

1. Validators signal how much of their staking rewards they are willing to re-allocate to Ethereum growth and security

2. Validators indicate recipient contracts that receive funds

sessions.title: The Hidden Costs of Running Ethereum on VMs
sessions.description: Most Ethereum teams upgraded from consumer hardware to cloud VMs, but VMs introduce hidden costs in performance, pricing, and reliability. This talk breaks down what Ethereum workloads need and how teams can build a stronger foundation.

sessions.title: Cote d'Azur Private Villa by FP Block
sessions.description: Private villa gathering on the Côte d'Azur. Organized by FP Block.

sessions.title: Maintaining Ethereum's Future
sessions.description: Non-technical overview of Ethereum core protocol development expectations short, medium, long-term with acceleration considerations.

sessions.title: Issuance Landscape
sessions.description: Ethereum’s issuance is at a key inflection point. This talk explores the current issuance landscape, the tradeoffs around validator incentives and network health, and frameworks for addressing it for long-term sustainability.

sessions.title: The road less travelled: 4 years of direct arithmetization of the EVM
sessions.description: Directly arithmetizing the EVM is hard, and even harder to maintain ... Or is it ? In this talk I will explain how our zk-EVM underwent massive changes, from speed running the London -> Osaka hard forks, switching fields to parallelizing the tracer.

sessions.title: How to be an AI demigod and not get rekt
sessions.description: AI is only as good as its prompter. Learn how we leverage AI, the Wonderland way.

sessions.title: Making Ethereum Post Quantum

sessions.description: MIPS ISA based zkVM proving Geth without Golang compiler modification, advancing client and prover diversity.

sessions.title: Learn to Build Verifiable, Trust-Minimized AI Agents for Real Users at Scale with LangGraph, x402, On-chain Delegation, and ERC-8004
sessions.description: Most AI agents today live inside black-box platforms, with unclear permissions and opaque execution. This workshop shows you how to build Trustless Agents as real products: autonomous economic actors that many humans and AI agents can coordinate with through clear endpoints, from Telegram bots and dapps to standard APIs (A2A, MCP), while users stay in control of what the agent can and cannot do on their behalf.

sessions.title: Token Engineering

sessions.description: Kohaku is a set of primitives that provides wallets with security and privacy. The project is focusing on privacy features and a browser extension that demonstrates the power of these features and targets power users.

sessions.title: Future-Proof Your Treasury: Post quantum Accounts for Kohaku wallet
sessions.description: Demonstration of post-quantum signature integration into Kohaku wallet

sessions.title: How Web3 Can Rebuild Truth on the Internet
sessions.description: Shared onchain knowledge layer with provenance and transparent editorial logic giving humans and AI trusted foundation.

sessions.title: Staking Quiz
sessions.description: Test your knowledge of Ethereum staking with this fun trivia game from Lamboshi and Jonathan.

sessions.title: Getting your EIP included is harder than getting a Ph.D.

sessions.title: Executing the Trustless Manifesto: Colibri Inside Ethereum’s Wallet SDK Kohaku
sessions.description: KOHAKU integrates the colibri.stateless client to remove RPC trust from wallets. Consensus and execution data are locally verified with minimal overhead. This brings the Trustless Manifesto into practice and enables wallets to operate fully trustless

sessions.title: When Clients Disagree: Picking a Side Safely
sessions.description: Client diversity makes Ethereum more resilient. But it also means that, from time to time, different clients disagree on which chain is valid, leading to a fork. When that happens, how can validators make sure they're voting for the correct side? This talk explores how multi-node validator clients (like Vero and Vouch) help validators navigate such situations safely by combining the views of multiple client implementations.

sessions.title: Preparing Blockchains for the Post-Quantum Era
sessions.description: Post-quantum cryptography as urgent blockchain concern given public permanent data expected valid for decades.

sessions.title: L1/L2 Scaling Roadmap
sessions.description: A discussion of the roadmap, progress, and direction for L1 and L2 scaling with Marius.

sessions.title: Asynchronous Execution in Practice
sessions.description: Asynchronous/delayed execution is a possible future for Ethereum to unlock massive blockchain throughput. It is already live on Monad and delivers the promised performance. But it also has deep impact on apps, users, and blockchain design.

sessions.title: Reasoning, And The Path To AGI
sessions.description: Current AI models can pattern match but not truly reason, and this limits what they are capable of. In this talk, I will outline how an open source market for the best reasoning agents will be essential in forging the path to true AGI.

sessions.title: Comparing & Benchmarking Post Quantum Digital Signature Schemes
sessions.description: Benchmarking post-quantum signature schemes (Falcon-512, Dilithium, SPHINCS+ and more) with real performance numbers, sizes, security assumptions, implementation risks, and zkVM verification overhead to guide production-ready choices.

sessions.description: LLMs generate code that appears correct yet omits critical security logic. In adversarial environments like DeFi, this is a structural failure, not a bug. This talk examines why GenAI cannot reason about adversaries, and why formal constraints are re

sessions.title: Privacy for Agents: Obfuscating Payment Trails in x402 & 8004
sessions.description: x402 and 8004 lean on public paper trails to connect agents. We describe the risks of this, and the potential of a future where x402/8004 can obfuscate usage patterns. We present some protocols that can make this future a reality.

sessions.title: Wonderland CTF
sessions.description: The largest Capture The Flag competition in crypto history. Organized by Wonderland.

sessions.title: 0x02 is not for Home Stakers! Right?
sessions.description: Since the introduction of the 0x02 validator withdrawal credentials (for compounding validators with up to 2048 ETH balance), this type of WCs hasn’t seen meaningful adoption among solo and home stakers. Let’s delve into the reasons and look at two innovations in the Ethereum space that might change it!

sessions.description: I've made a token bridge events dataset on dune, covering all appchain/L2 native bridges and 3rd party bridges. I focused on EVM chains but included most other chains too. I'll be presenting the dataset and some of the insights we can gather from it.

This talk breaks down the practical impacts of ePBS for validators and staking operators, from infrastructure simplification to new duties like the Payload Timeliness Committee, and what you need to do to prepare. We'll also explore how distributed validator technology turns major protocol upgrades from a risk into a resilience story.

sessions.title: Ethereum's decentralisation; five years into proof of stake
sessions.description: Review of Ethereum's PoS era with data on chain decentralization, followed by forward-looking recommendations for core protocol development.

sessions.description: CKKS-based FHE bringing computation on encrypted data to Ethereum enabling privacy-preserving contracts and confidential DeFi.

sessions.title: The State of Staking in 2026
sessions.description: In 2026, Rocket Pool is still Ethereum’s only fully permissionless liquid staking protocol. The protocol’s recent “Saturn One” upgrade made running a node even more accessible for over 1,500 global operators. Work has already begun on Saturn Two, which will bring more industry-leading improvements. Running a node in 2026 comes with unique challenges and opportunities. As a contributor to the leading decentralised staking protocol, Ken is unquely qualified to share an update on where staking is at today, and where it’s heading.

sessions.title: Connecting the East and West: Bridging AI Agents and Onchain Identity
sessions.description: Discover how multilingual AI agents with wallet-bound identities are changing Ethereum dApps. Live demo with autonomous agents using エーアイ.コム to perform onchain tasks, communicate, and evolve.

sessions.description: What if we valued Ethereum like the Internet? Indeed, Ethereum must be valued as a public goods infrastructure. This talk presents a three-lens framework revealing Ethereum's multi-trillion-dollar potential is far from today's reality.

sessions.description: Networking event by Cap in Cannes. Organized by Cap.

sessions.description: I will discuss ongoing work to safely snarkify the Ethereum protocol using formal verification. Expect to learn how formal verification delivers high-assurance code, and how we're using it on zkVMs, SNARKS, and the rest of the Ethereum protocol.

sessions.description: Isolating keys in dedicated devices is a done deal, but understanding what you sign is a critical issue. In this presentation we'll learn how to design a unified clear signing experience that could fit most resource constrained devices

sessions.description: Attestations alone aren’t enough - trust needs history, transparency, policies, and continuous verification.\nWe'll see how Oasis and ROFL turn static enclave quotes into a consensus-verified trust system.

sessions.title: Your guide to formal verification when machines write Lean proofs
sessions.description: Formal verification of software is cheaper than ever. AI agents are writing proofs in theorem provers like Lean. But how do you know what's proved in theorem provers corresponds to reality? Anyone who is interested should be able to compare definitions in theorem provers against what they know. The first good step is to use simpler programming languages that can be described in a few pages rather than languages that take tens of thousands of pages to describe.

sessions.description: Vyper and Huff for high-precision work.  Log tables, slide rules, IBM System 360, IEEE 754, ERC-20, Solady, and beyond.

sessions.description: Trillion Dollar Security (1TS) is a key ecosystem-wide effort spearheaded by Ethereum Foundation to upgrade Ethereum's security by educating, evaluating, enabling and effecting security challenges/opportunities across Ethereum’s technology stack.

sessions.description: A barrel of oil is identical whether first or last into the tank. In a block, first can mean millions; second can mean nothing. This talk introduces a new framework for the resource at the heart of Ethereum.

sessions.description: Naive tx replication for censorship resistance is costly. We use rateless erasure codes to shard txs across validators, reducing bandwidth 10x while ensuring strong probabilistic guarantees against adversarial censorship in MCP systems.

sessions.description: Cointelegraph's Cannes edition conference at Canopy by Hilton. Organized by Cointelegraph.

sessions.description: Overview of threshold encrypted mempools isolating design requirements and controlled MEV for geographic decentralisation.

sessions.title: From x*y=k to Ticks: Seeing Concentrated Liquidity on Uniswap v4 in Real Time
sessions.description: LPing went from x*y=k to ticks and ranges. I’ll show a real-time visual layer for concentrated liquidity, comparing pools across chains, tracking active liquidity near price, and surfacing LP behavior live. You’ll finally see CL.\n

sessions.title: Data Coprocessors For Sub-Second Blockchains.
sessions.description: The speaker discusses the latency crisis in the blockchain ecosystem and the solution, a data co-processor streaming arch that sits alongside nodes. This new approach allows for lower latency (sub-second data delivery), higher throughput, and cheaper

sessions.title: Improving Blockchain Security via the Open Source STABL Framework and Scalable Accountability
sessions.description: Recent research identifying Avalanche and Solana vulnerabilities with associated counter-measures and scalable accountability.

sessions.description: TheDAO is BACK! Don't be scared though. We will revisit its origins, explain how & why it re-emerged, why we had to hack it, AGAIN & explore how its treasury is being redirected to fund a new era of Ethereum security.

sessions.title: Future of Stateles Ethereum- Core Protocol Research & Optimization in Verkle trees
sessions.description: Fractional Verkle Trees with four critical performance pathologies in go-verkle: phantom node creation (+35% bloat), oversized database keys (+50% overhead), memory-copy deserialization (24× slower), and cross-implementation proof incompatibility.

sessions.description: The Linea zkEVM is unusual as it implements an EVM directly as a ZK circuit, rather than using a zkVM (e.g. RISC0, etc).  This talk will examine our approach in our more detail, and present a new approach to writing constraints we are adopting.

sessions.title: Designing Solidity Contracts That Don’t Fight Verification
sessions.description: Many Solidity contracts are written in ways that make them hard to reason about. This talk shows concrete patterns that break formal verification and how small architectural changes make contracts simpler, safer, and easier to prove correct.\n

sessions.description: Tomer Ganor Tech Lead Security Researcher at Certora, presents the security-centric design of Aave V4, exploring core architectural choices, their threat models, and how formal verification with the Certora Prover delivers robust, scalable guarantees

sessions.title: Making Sub-12s ZKPs on Ethereum Possible With a New Builder–Prover Design
sessions.description: Real-time zk proving pipeline where builders and provers work together enabling sub-12s Ethereum proving on affordable GPUs.

sessions.description: A bold reimagining of digital experiences where users control data and insights. I show how modern infrastructure and agency-first design can replace extractive platform models with user-owned, revocable, insight-centric applications.

sessions.title: Post Quantum Cryptography And The Future of Hardware
sessions.description: With the rise of quantum computing, the foundations of modern cryptography are being challenged, and our approach to hardware must evolve. This keynote examines how the quantum era will reshape cryptography and consumer hardware as we know it.

sessions.title: Uniswap v4 Hooks: A Security Guide for Builders and Breakers
sessions.description: Hooks unlock powerful customization in Uniswap v4, but they also introduce new attack surfaces. This talk covers the vulnerabilities that have already cost millions and how to build hooks that don't become the next headline.

sessions.description: Lighter bridges TradFi and DeFi with a ZK-powered liquidity layer for crypto and RWAs. Its US-domiciled structure ensures institutional credibility and compliance, unlocking institutional capital at scale as regulations evolve.

sessions.description: Smart account recovery requires trusting either centralized custodians or pre-selected guardians. This talk introduces a third path: using decentralized dispute resolution with randomly-selected jurors to enable recovery without any trusted relations

sessions.title: Common Vulnerabilities In Networking Protocols
sessions.description: Explore security flaws in networking infrastructure underlying blockchain protocols. Learn common vulnerabilities in UDP, TCP, WebSockets, HTTP, TLS, gRPC, and P2P protocols discovered during audits.

sessions.description: This talk dives into the current inefficiencies of block building algorithms, and shows how better handling of transaction nonces plus genetic algorithms can help find near optimal transaction sequences that increase block value.

sessions.title: Petanque

sessions.title: TEE as a Primitive for RWA & DeFi on Ethereum
sessions.description: How TEE enables confidential data processing, private tokens, and compliant off-chain workflows for RWA and DeFi on Ethereum, turning trusted execution into a core infrastructure primitive for institutional adoption.

sessions.title: An Offensive Security View to Cross Chain Bridge Security
sessions.description: A deep offensive-security walkthrough of cross-chain bridges , real attack paths, $2.5B+ bridge hacks, trust assumptions, lock-mint / burn-release models, network-layer weaknesses, and case studies showing how small misconfigs lead to catastrophic ex

sessions.title: Rethinking AMMs: Why Today’s Liquidity Design Won't Work  at Scale
sessions.description: The presentation will explore how traditional AMMs are no longer fit for purpose. As DeFi scales, the inefficiencies of traditional AMM design become a ball and chain on growth, that is until now.

sessions.title: Stateless Clients: Verifying Blockchain Data Without Trust or Infrastructure
sessions.description: How stateless clients eliminate RPC trust, enable on-device verification, and harden wallets, dApps, and agents against silent data-manipulation attacks. A practical walkthrough of proof-based blockchain access for Ethereum and L2s.

sessions.title: Million-Dollar Bugs, Zero Names
sessions.description: The talk will be about unpublished and interesting bugs that were submitted through Immunefi without project names (compliance). It focuses on anonymized code examples to show builders what can be found on deployed contracts/blockchains.

sessions.title: Strengthening Oracle Security in 2026: Key Principles for Builders
sessions.description: Framework for selecting, configuring, and integrating oracles with checklist, real-world examples, security considerations.

sessions.title: Rethinking PeerDAS: Transparent, Post-Quantum DAS with FRI
sessions.description: This talk explores using FRI as DAS in PeerDAS, how to deploying it into the consensus layer, and how multi-verifier settings can enable trusted-setup-free, post-quantum secure DAS via distributed verification of availability on Ethereum.

sessions.title: Technical Priorities in Ethereum’s Enterprise Adoption Arc
sessions.description: Enterprises are moving onchain and they’re coming to Ethereum and L2s like Optimism. I’m in the trenches with CTOs and decision makers as they evaluate tech stacks and where to deploy. I’ll share my insights on how the Ethereum ecosystem can win.

sessions.title: Ethereum Layer Two Client Similarity: Geth
sessions.description: Many L2 networks on Ethereum use Geth to build blocks. We will look at the Geth codebase in order to understand changes made and how these codebases evolve. This is important to determine how updates are propagated and establish dependencies.
